observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to the following: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Health Office to maintain complete stock cards of the inventories and conduct periodic reconciliation with the ledger cards; b. Form an inventory committee and conduct physical count every six (6) months, prepare a Report of Physical Count of Inventories and submit it to the Office of the Auditor; and c. Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries to restore back to the inventory account the undistributed/unconsumed items at the end of the year.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the concerned Municipal Officials facilitate full liquidation of fund transfers from other Government Agencies in accordance with the agreements entered thereto.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following: a. Require the Municipal Officials and Employees to immediately complete the liquidation of the cash advances and ensure strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and b. Require the Municipal Accountant withhold the salaries of concerned personnel who failed to comply with the instructions on liquidation of their cash advances as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Management: a. Immediately complete the liquidation of the cash advances and ensure strict compliance of Sections 5 and 8 of COA Circular No. 97-002; b. Designate other bonded employees with no outstanding cash advance as Special Disbursing Officer (SDO) for specific undertakings to prevent simultaneous or overlapping of cash advance to one particular SDO; c. Require the Municipal Treasurer with Other Accountable Officers to reconcile with the Municipal Accountant their respective books of accounts at least once in every quarter in compliance with Section 6 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and d. Require the Municipal Accountant withhold the salaries of concerned personnel who failed to comply with the instructions on liquidation of their cash advances as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant send demand letters regularly for the immediate submission of liquidation reports by the concerned NGOs/POs and exhaust all possible remedies to require the concerned NGOs/POs to prepare and submit Fund Utilization Report. If found futile, the agency may request for the write-off of the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant or her authorized representative submit all DVs for cash advance for intelligence fund and confidential activities/projects with complete required supporting documents, within seven (7) working days from release of the check to COA ATL in the manner prescribed under the guidelines and procedures on the entitlement, release, use, reporting, and audit of confidential and/or intelligence fund under the aforementioned Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign the Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality of Hamtic to ascertain its ownership over said properties and these Torrens Title should be properly kept by the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer. Proper reconciliation of cost and inventory of land should be undertaken to reflect an accurate book balance.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the
Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of all Property,
Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and prepare the required
report thereof. Also, we recommended and the Management agreed that the
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile PPE items in
the Reports of Physical Count of PPE with the accounting records and effect
necessary adjustments to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial
statements.

We reiterated and the Management agreed to the following
recommendations:
1. Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Health
Office to maintain complete stock cards of the inventories and conduct
periodic reconciliation with the ledger cards;
2. Form an inventory committee and conduct physical count every six (6)
months, prepare a Report of Physical Count of Inventories and submit it
to the Office of the Auditor; and
3. Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting
entries to restore back to the inventory account the
undistributed/unconsumed items at the end of the year.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief
Executive:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer and other collecting officers to
accomplish the ROCDs every close of each business day;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit regularly all collections
intact as required by the rules and regulations to prevent
misappropriation, theft and loss of funds and impose administrative
sanctions if warranted; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with
other Accountable Officers to reconcile their respective books of
accounts at least quarterly with complete supporting documents so that
collections will be reported regularly and will be recorded to come up
with a fair presentation of the Financial Statements and accurate
financial condition and results of operations of the Municipality for a
given period of time.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to transfer the unexpended or unobligated of the QRF and
DRRMF-MOOE from the Due to Other Funds - General Fund to the Trust
Liability – DRRMF under the Trust Fund through the below proposed
journal entry:

Due to Other Funds - General Fund ₱10,206,950.23
Trust Liability – DRRMF ₱10,206,950.23

We recommended and the Management agreed to conduct a complete physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and prepare the required report thereof. Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile PPE items in the Reports of Physical Count of PPE with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE secures the authority from the Sanggunian for the utilization of the lump sum appropriation, appropriated under the 20% development fund. We further recommended and the Management also agreed that the MBO exercise prudence in the utilization of the executive budget and comply with the requirements by the Budget Operations Manual prior to submission to the Sanggunian for authorization through enactment of Appropriation Ordinance.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Administrator countersign the checks to be issued in compliance with Section 345 of Republic Act No. 7160 otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended and the Management agreed that: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer and other collecting officers to accomplish the ROCDs every close of each business day; b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit regularly all collections intact as required by the rules and regulations to prevent misappropriation, theft and loss of funds and impose administrative sanctions if warranted; and c. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with other Accountable Officers to reconcile their respective books of accounts at least quarterly with complete supporting documents so that collections will be reported regularly and will be recorded to come up with a fair presentation of the Financial Statements and accurate financial condition and results of operations of the Municipality for a given period of time.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly adhere to the submission of the duly accomplished Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation on audit observations and recommendations embodied in the AAR. All columns in the form be properly accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We recommended that the Management revisit the granting of her Fidelity Bond to all accountable officers to strictly monitor the proper amount of cash accountability that can cover the government funds in case of defalcations, shortages and unrelieved losses. Otherwise, the Municipal Treasurer limit the cash advances granted to Disbursing Officer up to the maximum cash accountability based on their respective approved amount of Fidelity Bond.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant together with Accountable Officers regularly reconcile the cashbook at least once in every quarter in compliance with Section 6 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended the following: a. The Management review the lease contracts and make necessary action/amendment to align with the Municipal Tax Ordinance; b. The Management execute a contract of lease for all stalls to secure the enforcement of the lease payments; c. Exert more effort in collecting delinquent rental fees and issue notices of demand for rental payments inclusive of penalties from all delinquent market stallholders as soon as the payment falls due in accordance with its power to create sources of revenues; and d. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer strictly implement the pertinent provisions of Municipal Tax Ordinance No. 03 Series 2013 and Republic Act No. 7160.

We recommended the following: a. The Municipality henceforth, procure its rice requirements with the NFA except a waiver is issued consistent with the provision of EO 51; and b. Enter into a Memorandum of Agreement with the NFA to facilitate the efficient and economical procurement of its rice requirements.
We recommended that the Management ensure the completeness of supporting documents prior to payment and strictly adhere with Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 01.
We recommended that the Management ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents before processing the payment.

We recommended that the Management require all the Department Heads to submit the POs with other supporting documents to the Auditors for review and inspection, within the prescribed period and strictly adhere with Section 3 of COA Circular 2009-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the members of the Inspection Team ensure the Acceptance and Inspection Reports are properly filled out with complete information about the procured items.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to monitor the implementation of the aforesaid infrastructure projects under the Health Facilities Enhancement Program. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive enforce administrative sanctions and penalties against the contractor in case of delays without valid cause.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness and propriety of the documentary requirements in equipment rental disbursements.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor, as head of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), ensure the full implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities of the Municipality as incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget. We also recommended that the GFPS – Technical Working Group check the accuracy of the information and computation indicated in the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects/activities for the benefits of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended the following: a. The Municipal Solid Waste Management Focal Person follow up the status of the approval of the plan; b. Material Recovery Facility should be provided or established by the Municipality in the Hamtic New Public Market to promote segregation of waste through recycling and composting.

We recommended that the agency hire additional accounting personnel to assist the Municipal Accountant to handle the accounting tasks and prepare the required reports in order to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units Volume I and pertinent COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the timely conduct of audit.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and prepare the required report thereof;
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile PPE items in the Reports of Physical Count of PPE with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:
a.	Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Health Office to maintain complete stock cards of the inventories and conduct periodic reconciliation with the ledger cards;
b.	Form an inventory committee and conduct physical count every six (6) months, prepare a Report of Physical Count of Inventories and submit it to the Office of the Auditor; and
c.	Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries to restore back to the inventory account the undistributed/unconsumed items at the end of the year.

We recommended the following: 
The Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit regularly all collections intact as required by the rules and regulations to prevent misappropriation, theft and loss of funds and impose administrative sanctions if warranted; and

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with other Accountable Officers to reconcile their respective books of accounts at least quarterly so that collections will be reported regularly and will be recorded to come up with a fair presentation of the Financial Statements and accurate financial condition and results of operations of the Municipality for a given period of time.

We recommended that the Management regularly revisit the granting of Fidelity Bond to all accountable officers to strictly monitor the proper amount of cash accountability that can cover the government funds in case of defalcations, shortages and unrelieved losses. Otherwise, the Municipal Treasurer limit the cash advances granted to Disbursing Officer up to the maximum cash accountability based on their respective approved Fidelity Bond.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately require and demand the complete liquidation of the long outstanding cash advances and ensure strict compliance with Sections 4, 5 and 8 of COA Circular No. 97-002. 

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer together with Accountable Officers regularly reconcile the cashbook at least once in every quarter in compliance with Section 6 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

The Municipal Accountant strictly monitor the outstanding cash advances and refrain/stop the granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees without liquidating their outstanding cash advances. 

The Municipal Accountant withhold the AO’s salary whenever there are outstanding cash advances on or before January 20 of the current year.

We recommended the following: 
The Municipality henceforth, procure its rice requirements with the NFA except a waiver is issued consistent with the provision of EO 51; and

Enter into a Memorandum of Agreement with the NFA to facilitate the efficient and economical procurement of its rice requirements.
We recommended that the Management ensure the completeness of supporting documents prior to payment and strictly adhere with Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 01.
We recommended that the Management ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents before processing the payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), ensure the full implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities of the Municipality as incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We also recommended that the GFPS – Technical Working Group check the accuracy of the information and computation indicated in the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive require concerned personnel or committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials at least once a year and reflect the result in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and

The Accounting Office and the Supply and Property Unit maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively and periodically reconcile their balances for accurate presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects/ activities for the benefits of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant send demand letters regularly for the immediate submission of liquidation reports by the concerned NGOs/POs and exhaust all possible remedies to require the concerned NGOs/POs to prepare and submit Fund Utilization Report.  If found futile, the agency may request for the write-off of the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council.

We recommended that the agency hire additional accounting personnel to assist the Municipal Accountant to handle the accounting tasks and prepare the required reports in order to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units Volume I and pertinent COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the timely conduct of audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant or her authorized representative submit all DVs for cash advance for intelligence fund and confidential activities/projects with complete required supporting documents, within seven (7) working days from release of the check to COA ATL in the manner prescribed under the guidelines and procedures on the entitlement, release, use, of confidential and/or intelligence fund under the aforementioned Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign the Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality of Hamtic to ascertain its ownership over said properties and these Torrens Title should be properly kept by the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer. Proper reconciliation of cost and inventory of land should be undertaken to reflect an accurate book balance.

We recommended that the Management require all the Department Heads to submit the POs with other supporting documents within the prescribed period and strictly adhere with Section 3 of COA Circular 2009-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the members of the Inspection Team ensure the Acceptance and Inspection Reports are properly filled out with complete information about the procured items.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to monitor the implementation and ensure the completion of the aforesaid infrastructure projects under the Health Facilities Enhancement Program.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive enforce administrative sanctions and penalties against the contractor in case of delays without valid cause.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness and propriety of the documentary requirements in equipment rental disbursements.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive require concerned personnel or committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials at least once a year and reflect the result in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and

The Accounting Office and the Supply and Property Unit maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively and periodically reconcile their balances for accurate presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant send demand letters regularly for the immediate submission of liquidation reports by the concerned NGOs/POs and exhaust all possible remedies to require the concerned NGOs/POs to prepare and submit Fund Utilization Report.  If found futile, the agency may request for the write-off of the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant or her authorized representative submit all DVs for cash advance for intelligence fund and confidential activities/projects with complete required supporting documents, within seven (7) working days from release of the check to COA ATL in the manner prescribed under the guidelines and procedures on the entitlement, release, use, of confidential and/or intelligence fund under the aforementioned Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and prepare the required report thereof.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile PPE items in the reports of physical count of PPE with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments to present fairly the PPE account balances in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare and attach the Statement of Time Elapsed and Percentage of Work Accomplished to every progress billing of infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance one year after the contract completion in order to observe the defects liability period within which the contractor assumes the responsibility to undertake the repair works, at his own expense, of any damage to the infrastructure on account of the use of materials of inferior quality.
We recommended that the BAC Secretariat ensure the required information in the POs are completely filled out.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the members of the Inspection Team ensure the Acceptance and Inspection Reports are properly filled out with complete information about the procured items.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to monitor the implementation and ensure the completion of the aforesaid infrastructure projects under the Health Facilities Enhancement Program.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive enforce administrative sanctions and penalties against the contractor in case of delays without valid cause.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer pay the salaries and other benefits directly to the concerned municipal officials and employees or to their immediate family members with proper authorization and identification.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness and propriety of the documentary requirements in equipment rental disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), ensure the full implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities of the Municipality as incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget.
We also recommended that the GFPS – Technical Working Group check the accuracy of the information and computation indicated in the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee with sufficient number of personnel who can effectively and efficiently conduct the periodic physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) owned or controlled by the Municipality and prepare the report on the physical count for each group of PPE; and

The Municipal Accountant reconcile PPE items in the reports of physical count of PPE per group with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive require the BAC to include in the Bid Data Sheet/Request for Quotation a provision requiring the prospective bidders/suppliers to specify in their bid offers/price quotations the brand names of the goods they are going to offer and to include this requirement in the evaluation of bids or quotations; and

The Local Chief Executive ensure that all required information in the Purchase Order are completely filled out by the concerned personnel.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive, as the HoPE, use the Direct Retail Purchase method in its procurement of POL products, wherein delegation of authority to the end-user is permitted; and

The OIC – Municipal Accountant ensure that the Annual Procurement Plan and the resolution delegating such authority, if used, be attached as additional supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers relative thereto.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee to regularly update the Annual Procurement Plan and ensure that the information therein such as mode of procurement, schedule and source of funds for each procurement project are complete.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure the technical specifications of the items to be procured are specific and detailed to properly determine the reasonableness of the price and the propriety of the goods delivered.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials to:

Formulate the LGU’s APP in the GPPB prescribed format per project and program with complete information;

Submit the APP to the GPPB TSO every January including the supplemental APP; and

Attach the authenticated APP to the disbursement vouchers for every payment of procurement activities of the Municipality.
We recommended that the management strictly comply with the documentary requirements concerning payments of goods and services in adherence to COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other related issuances.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Municipal Engineer properly plans the programs and projects to be undertaken by the municipality to ensure effective and efficient allocation of resources as well as timely implementation of project (Fully Implemented in CY 2020); and

Hire additional engineers to help the Municipal Engineer in monitoring and implementing the projects; and

The Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects/ activities for the benefits of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We therefore recommended that:

The Municipal Engineer strictly monitor the timeline as specified in the conditions of the contract to impose the necessary liquidated damages to the contractor; and

The Municipal Engineer further review the provisions of R.A 9184 as to the accountability or liability of the contractor on the repair of the damaged wall due to force majeure or unforeseen events.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board through the Solid Waste Management Focal Person follow up the status of the approval of the plan;



Material Recovery Facility should be provided or established by the Municipality in the Hamtic New Public Market to promote segregation of waste through recycling and composting; and



The MENRO-Designate, in coordination with other offices of the Municipality remind the residents of the Municipality not to use open burning as the mode of garbage disposal (Fully Implemented in CY 2020).

We recommended that the agency hire additional accounting personnel to assist the Municipal Accountant to handle the accounting tasks and prepare the required reports in order to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units Volume I and pertinent COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the timely conduct of audit.

We recommended that the Head of Agency require early liquidation of the cash advances, otherwise, enforce the provision of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on withholding of Accountable Officer’s salary on the ground of his/her failure to liquidate his/her cash advances at the end of the fiscal year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign the Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality of Hamtic to ascertain its ownership over said properties and these Torrens Title should be properly kept by the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer. Proper reconciliation of cost and inventory of land should be undertaken to reflect an accurate book balance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant send demand letters regularly for the immediate submission of liquidation reports by the concerned NGOs/POs and exhaust all possible remedies to require the concerned NGOs/POs to prepare and submit Fund Utilization Report.  If found futile, the agency may request for the write-off of the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant or her authorized representative submit all DVs for cash advance for intelligence fund and confidential activities/projects with complete required supporting documents, within seven (7) working days from release of the check to COA ATL in the manner prescribed under the guidelines and procedures on the entitlement, release, use, of confidential and/or intelligence fund under the aforementioned Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee with sufficient number of personnel who can effectively and efficiently conduct the periodic physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) owned or controlled by the Municipality and prepare the report on the physical count for each group of PPE; and
The Municipal Accountant reconcile PPE items in the reports of physical count of PPE per group with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive require concerned personnel or committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials at least once a year and reflect the result in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and
The Accounting Office and the Supply and Property Unit maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively and periodically reconcile their balances for accurate presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive require the BAC to include in the Bid Data Sheet/Request for Quotation a provision requiring the prospective bidders/suppliers to specify in their bid offers/price quotations the brand names of the goods they are going to offer and to include this requirement in the evaluation of bids or quotations; and
The Local Chief Executive ensure that all required information in the Purchase Order are completely filled out by the concerned personnel.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive, as the HoPE, use the Direct Retail Purchase method in its procurement of POL products, wherein delegation of authority to the end-user is permitted; and
The OIC – Municipal Accountant ensure that the Annual Procurement Plan and the resolution delegating such authority, if used, be attached as additional supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers relative thereto.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee to regularly update the Annual Procurement Plan and ensure that the information therein such as mode of procurement, schedule and source of funds for each procurement project are complete.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure the technical specifications of the items to be procured are specific and detailed to properly determine the reasonableness of the price and the propriety of the goods delivered.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials to:
Formulate the LGU’s APP in the GPPB prescribed format per project and program with complete information;
Submit the APP to the GPPB TSO every January including the supplemental APP; and
Attach the authenticated APP to the disbursement vouchers for every payment of procurement activities of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Management strictly comply with the documentary requirements concerning payments of goods and services in adherence to COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other related issuances.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Municipal Engineer properly plans the programs and projects to be undertaken by the municipality to ensure effective and efficient allocation of resources as well as timely implementation of project;

Hire additional engineers to help the Municipal Engineer in monitoring and implementing the projects; and

The Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects/ activities for the benefits of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We therefore recommended that:

The Municipal Engineer strictly monitor the timeline as specified in the conditions of the contract to impose the necessary liquidated damages to the contractor; and

The Municipal Engineer further review the provisions of R.A 9184 as to the accountability or liability of the contractor on the repair of the damaged wall due to force majeure or unforeseen events.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.  Close monitoring and supervision should be diligently performed by the official and staff to achieve the objective of transparency.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board through the Solid Waste Management Focal Person follow up the status of the approval of the plan;



Material Recovery Facility should be provided or established by the Municipality in the Hamtic New Public Market to promote segregation of waste through recycling and composting; and




The MENRO-Designate, in coordination with other offices of the Municipality remind the residents of the Municipality not to use open burning as the mode of garbage disposal.

We recommended that the agency hire additional accounting personnel to assist the Municipal Accountant to handle the accounting tasks and prepare the required reports in order to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units Volume I and pertinent COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the timely conduct of audit.

We recommended that the Head of Agency require early liquidation of the cash advances, otherwise, enforce the provision of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on withholding of Accountable Officer’s salary on the ground of his/her failure to liquidate his/her cash advances at the end of the fiscal year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign the Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality of Hamtic to ascertain its ownership over said properties and these Torrens Title should be properly kept by the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer. Proper reconciliation of cost and inventory of land should be undertaken to reflect an accurate book balance.

We recommended that: 
The inventory committee conduct the physical inventory of all property and equipment owned by the LGU;

Prepare promptly the RPCPPE to be used as basis of the Municipal Accountant in booking up unrecorded PPE; and 

The Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of PPE accounts with accounting records and effect necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to come up with more reliable account balance of the PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant or her authorized representative submit  all DVs for cash advance for intelligence fund and confidential activities/projects with complete required supporting documents, within seven (7) working days from release of the check to COA ATL in the manner prescribed under  the guidelines and procedures on the entitlement, release, use, of confidential and/or intelligence fund under the aforementioned  Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended that the Head of Agency require early liquidation of the cash advances, otherwise, enforce the provision of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on withholding of Accountable Officer’s salary on the ground of his/her failure to liquidate his/her cash advances at the end of the fiscal year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign the Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of the lots in the name of the Municipality of Hamtic to ascertain its ownership over said properties and these Torrens Title should be properly kept by the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer. Proper reconciliation of cost and inventory of land should be undertaken to reflect an accurate book balance.

We recommended that:
The agency create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of all property and equipment owned by the LGU;
Prepare promptly the RPCPPE to be used as basis of the Municipal Accountant in booking up unrecorded PPE; and
The Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of PPE accounts with accounting records and effect necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to come up with more reliable balances of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Municipal Engineer properly plans the programs and projects to be undertaken by the municipality to ensure effective and efficient allocation of resources as well as timely implementation of project.
Hire additional engineers to help the Municipal Engineer in monitoring and implementing the projects.
The Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects/activities for the benefits of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of  DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We therefore recommended that:
The Municipal Engineer strictly monitor the timeline as specified in the conditions of the contract to impose the necessary liquidated damages to the contractor.
The Municipal Engineer further review the provisions of R.A 9184 as to the accountability or liability of  the contractor on the repair of the damaged wall due to force majeure or unforeseen events.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office strictly follow the provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.  Close monitoring  and supervision should be diligently  performed by the official and staff to achieve the objective of transparency.

We recommended the following:
The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board through the Solid Waste Management Focal Person follow up the status of the approval of the plan.
Material Recovery Facility should be provided or established by the Municipality in the Hamtic New Public Market to promote segregation of waste through recycling and composting.
The MENRO-Designate, in coordination with other offices of the Municipality remind the residents of the Municipality not to use open burning as the mode of garbage disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant send demand letters regularly for the immediate submission of liquidation reports by the concerned NGOs/POs and exhaust all possible remedies to require the concerned NGOs/POs to prepare and submit Fund Utilization Report.  If found futile, the agency may request for the write-off of the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant send demand letters regularly for the immediate submission of liquidation reports by the concerned NGOs/POs and exhaust all possible remedies to require the concerned NGOs/POs to prepare and submit Fund Utilization Reports.  If found futile, he may request for the write-off of the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances to those officers and employees who are in active service.



For those officers and employees who are no longer in service, the Municipal Accountant exhaust all possible remedies to collect and demand liquidation.  If found to be fruitless, request for the write-off of the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipality completely develop and maintain a Gender and Development (GAD) database for gender statistics and sex-disaggregation data as required under Republic Act (RA) 9710 and its Implementing Rules  and Regulations (IRR), Memorandum Circular No. 2014-05 of the Philippine Commission (PCW) and DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board through the designated MENRO expedite the completion and submission of the plan for its immediate approval as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.

We recommended that additional training courses for personnel and the involvement of community based DRRM shall be included in the Municipality’s LDRRM Plan as required under Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 2012 and Section 2 of Rule 10 of the Implementing Rules and Regulation (IRR) of Republic Act 10121.

We recommended  that the agency hire additional accounting personnel to assist the Municipal Accountant to handle the accounting tasks and prepare the required reports in order to  comply  with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units Volume I and pertinent COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the timely conduct of audit.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC).

We recommended that: 
The inventory committee conduct the physical inventory of all property and equipment owned by the LGU;

Prepare promptly the RPCPPE to be used as basis of the Municipal Accountant in booking up unrecorded PPE; and 

The Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of PPE accounts with accounting records and effect necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to come up with more reliable account balance of the PPE in the financial statements.

No inventory conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Treasurer cancels all checks which have been unclaimed by the payee and became stale and outstanding for more than six months.  For those checks which became stale in the hands of the payee or holder, send written notice to surrender the same for cancellation and replacement.

For those checks that not have been presented in the bank for payment and became stale, the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts  to correct  the balance of  cash in bank and payable  accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended  that the agency hire additional accounting personnel to assist the Municipal Accountant to handle the accounting tasks and prepare the required reports in order to  comply  with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units Volume I and pertinent COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the timely conduct of audit.

Delayed submission of reports.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the abovementioned observations, we recommended the following:

The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly follow the provisions of the Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 and, as much as practicable, adopt competitive bidding in the procurement of the Municipality, otherwise, adopt alternative modes of procurement that is most appropriate for the transaction supported with relevant documentation.  

The management indicate the complete details/technical specifications in every Purchase Request and Purchase Orders in order to make sure that the goods/services procured are of good quality and meet the needs/requirements of the end-users.  Likewise, require that suppliers indicate the brands that they offer in their quotations.


The Municipal Accountant thoroughly check the Disbursement Vouchers for completeness of signatures and attachments in succeeding transactions before submitting to the Office of the Auditor in order to substantiate the validity of the transactions and to facilitate post-audit.

In view of the abovementioned observations, we recommended the following:

The management refrain from charging the Special Education Fund (SEF) for expenditures that do not fall within the specific allowable expenditures provided by the Letter of Instructions No. 1462 and to priority expenses stipulated by DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998, 01-A s. 2000 and 01-B s. of 2001.

The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly follow the provisions of the Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 and, as much as practicable, adopt competitive bidding in the procurement of the Municipality, otherwise, adopt alternative mode of procurement that is most appropriate for the transaction.  The BAC be also vigilant in evaluating the quoted price of the suppliers/contractors to make sure that only those that are within the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) are processed and awarded with the contract.

The management indicate the complete details/technical specifications in every Purchase Request and Purchase Orders in order to make sure that the goods/services procured are of good quality and meet the needs/requirements of the end-users.  

The Municipal Accountant thoroughly check the Disbursement Vouchers for completeness of signatures and attachments in succeeding transactions before submitting to the Office of the Auditor in order to substantiate the validity of the transactions and to facilitate post-audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant or her authorized representative submit  all DVs for cash advance for intelligence fund and confidential activities/projects with complete required supporting documents, within seven (7) working days from release of the check to COA ATL in the manner prescribed under  the guidelines and procedures on the entitlement, release, use, of confidential and/or intelligence fund under the aforementioned  Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

In view of the abovementioned observations, we recommended the following:

The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) be used solely for DRRM programs, projects and activities as provided for in RA 10121 and its IRR, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG MC No. 2012-73.  Projects similar to those provided by the aforementioned law and circulars may also be funded out of the LDRRMF as long as they pertain to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness and response.

The management refrain from using the LDRRMF to fund for the Blood Indigency Program.  The importance of the said program is recognized, thus management look/create separate appropriation for such program to ensure its continuity.
We recommended that the Municipal SWM Board through the MENRO follow up the status of the approval of the plan as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Finding No. 6 AAR 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality through its GAD Focal Person prepare GAD Plan and Budget and prepare its Accomplishment Report in accordance with Section C.6 item 2 of the Joint Circular 2013-001 of the PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA.

We recommended that the Municipal Account follow the pertual inventory method in recording purchases of supplies and materials. Maintain Stock/Property Cards for each inventory  to enable  reconciliation of the inventory  records with property records to prove the correctness of the Inventory account.
observations.reason: Procurement of supplies is not centralized or undertaken by the designated GSO.  Thus, monitoring and updating of stock cards is least likely to be practicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare updated subsidiary ledgers for Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs as well as all other controlling accounts as required under Section 114 of PD 1445.
observations.reason: Subsidiary Ledgers were maintained for both accounts.  However, not all of the declared balances were accounted for and reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant book up the liquidations affecting the Cash Disbursing Officer account and reconcile the same with the account balance of the Disbursing Officer’s cashbook
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare updated subsidiary ledgers for Current Liabilities and keep the supporting documents intact for reference and audit purposes.
observations.reason: The Accountant needs more time to collate documents and analyze the account in order to come up with an accurate breakdown of the accounts. No available documents to substantiate/ support claims especially for balances that cannot be reconciled with subsidiary records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of Agency require early liquidation of the cash advances, otherwise, enforce the provision of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on withholding of Accountable Officer’s salary on the ground of his/her failure to liquidate his/her cash advances at the end of the fiscal year.

Proper reconciliation of cost and inventory of land should be undertaken to reflect an accurate book balance.

After the reconciliation the Municipal Accountant effect necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to come up with a more reliable account balance of the PPE in the financial statements.

The management indicate the complete details/technical specifications in every Purchase Request and Purchase Orders in order to make sure that the goods/services procured are of good quality and meet the needs/requirements of the end-users.  Likewise, require that suppliers indicate the brands that they offer in their quotations.
The Municipal Accountant thoroughly check the Disbursement Vouchers for completeness of signatures and attachments in succeeding transactions before submitting to the Office of the Auditor in order to substantiate the validity of the transactions and to facilitate post-audit.

The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) be used solely for DRRM programs, projects and activities as provided for in RA 10121 and its IRR, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG MC No. 2012-73. Finding was first raised in AAR 2013, Finding No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer be directed to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units and COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the conduct of timely audit.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of Agency take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: The withholding of salaries was suspended because the average net pay of employees are not sufficient without violating the minimum P3,000 net pay required by GAA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the employees/personnel to attach taxi metered  receipts to support claim for taxi fare of more than P75.00 as required under COA Circular No. 96-005 dated May 2, 1996 and  Paragraph 27 of LTFRB Memorandum Circular Number 2011-004 dated May 27, 2011.

We recommended that the Municipal Account follow the pertual inventory method in recording purchases of supplies and materials. Maintain Stock/Property Cards for each inventory  to enable  reconciliation of the inventory  records with property records to prove the correctness of the Inventory account.
observations.reason: Procurement of supplies is not centralized or undertaken by designated GSO.  Thus, monitoring and updating of stock cards is least likely to be practicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare updated subsidiary ledgers for Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs as well as all other controlling accounts as required under Section 114 of PD 1445.
observations.reason: Subsidiary Ledgers were maintained for both accounts.  However, not all of the declared balances were accounted for and reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant book up the liquidations affecting the Cash Disbursing Officer account and reconcile the same with the account balance of the Disbursing Officer’s cashbook

We recommended that the Head of Agency take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances. Personnel who have copies of their liquidation documents may submit the documents to liquidate their cash advances. For those whose liquidation documents were rotten/ eaten by termites in the storage area, of the deceased and of those who are no longer in service, the Municipal Accountant may apply for writing off of the unliquidated balances.
observations.reason: The withholding of salaries was suspended because the average net pay of employees are not sufficient without violating the minimum P3,000.00 net pay required by GAA.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare updated subsidiary ledgers for Current Liabilities and keep the supporting documents intact for reference and audit purposes.
observations.reason: The Accountant needs more time to collate documents and analyze the account in order to come up with an accurate breakdown of the accounts. No available documents to substantiate/ support claims especially for balances that cannot be reconciled with subsidiary records.
